entry.title: Issue Adding or Removing Proxies from Product in SAP API Management – Screen Keeps Loading
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m facing an issue in SAP API Management and would appreciate your help or insights.

I’ve created several API Proxies for my SAP CPI system and also created a Product. When I try to assign proxies to the Product:

  • For some proxies, it works fine, and I’m able to add them successfully.

  • For other proxies, the screen keeps loading indefinitely and never completes the assignment process.

Here’s what I’ve tried so far:

✅ I recreated and redeployed the problematic proxies.
✅ I tested the proxies directly in the browser — they work fine when called.
✅ I tried performing the assignment in Incognito mode and in different browsers.
✅ I even tried removing previously assigned proxies, but the UI again keeps loading endlessly during the remove operation.

Despite all this, the issue persists. It seems like there might be an internal problem in API Management, possibly some stuck metadata or backend inconsistency, but I’m not certain.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue?

  • Is there any workaround to force-remove a proxy from a Product (e.g. via REST APIs)?

  • Is there a way to clean up potentially stuck objects in API Management?

  • Any logs or places to check for deeper troubleshooting?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your support.

#

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) - Generate ESG Reports With AI Application
entry.content:
As part of the June 2025 release, SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) has introduced a new out-of-the-box functionality, Generate ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) reports with AI application. With the Generate ESG Reports with AI app, the ESG reporting process is automated across the selected periods. It uses the ESG data that has already been imported into SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) and creates the reports based on the imported data. The AI generated reports are available to download in the .pdf format.

In this blog, I will outline how to define the content for custom templates based on set of facts, i.e.,  available metrics or reference facts, and how to generate ESG reports by leveraging AI.

Mini Note:

If you want to understand the nitty-gritty of SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT) out-of-the-box functionalities and its Integration with SAP EHSM-EM (Emission Management), check-out my blog posts listed below.

  1. European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) 
  2. SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHS Environment Management 
  3. Addendum to SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHSM-EM 
  4. SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT) Metric Management-Basic Custom Metric & Standard Custom Metric 
  5. Exporting and Importing Custom Objects and CPE Packages in SAP Sustainability Control Tower 
  6. Managing Qualitative Metrics in SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT) for ESRS and BRSR Framework 
  7. SAP S/4HANA EHS : Environment Management (Emissions Management) – Part:1 (Master Data Entities) 
  8. SAP S/4HANA EHS : Environment Management (Emissions Management) – Part:2 (Identifying & Preparing Compliance Requirement) 
  9. SAP S/4HANA EHS : Environment Management (Emissions Management) – Part:3 (Compliance Scenario Activities) 

 

Let us get started...why wait?...

Generate ESG Reports With AI Application

 

Let us take look at the "Prerequisites" before we dive in

  1. ESG specific data must have been imported into SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT)
  2. Must have the roles, i.e., SUS_SCT_AI_Reporting_Assistant_Manager & SUS_SCT_AI_Reporting_Assistant_Viewer

ESG Data Imported into Sustainability Control Tower

 

For instance, I have created a template named "ESG Report Template for Calcination & Power"  to generate an ESG report for the above imported data using the AI application.

ESG Report Template for Calcination & Power Business Lines

 

let us use the "Create"  option to set-up a tailored custom template for ESG metrics and reference facts.  For Instance, I have added sections and subsections to align with the ESG pillars to have the template structure. We can rename, remove the sections and subsections at any point of time.

Sections and Subsections of ESG report

 

Now, let us start filling the sections with the content. To add the text to the "carbon emissions profile" , I have built the content by adding "Reference Facts" and " Metrics". We can add maximum 30 facts for each section or subsection.

Pillar: Environment

We can choose the "Reference facts" from the predefined facts, and metrics can be chosen from the available SAP provided quantitative metrics. The text  in this section will be generated by AI (Artificial Intelligence) for the metrics defined in the "Manage Metrics App".

Assignment of Reference Facts & Metrics to generate text for the Carbon Emissions Profile

 

Now, let us use the option " Generate" to preview the content for the above assigned reference facts and metrics.

 

AI generated text for the Carbon Emissions Profile

 

Now, let us generate charts for the "Carbon Emissions Profile". 

 

 

Following the successful assignment of the charts, use the option "Generate" charts for each metric.

 

AI generated graph for the GHG Emissions by GHG Scope & Business Locations

 

AI generated graph for the GHG Emissions by Business Country

 

AI generated graph for the Location & Market based Emissions

 

Now, let us create tables to fill out emissions metrics details by leveraging AI. Use the option " Add Table" to assign the required metrics tables.

For Instance, I have chosen two tables to represent  GHG Emissions (CO2e) & GHG Emissions Reduction Targets (CO2e).

 

 

Following the successful addition of the tables, use the option "Generate" to fill out the details for the selected table.

AI generated table for Scope-1 Emissions

 

AI generated table for Scope-2 & Scope-3 Emissions

 

In a similar manner, let us assign the reference facts and metrics to generate text , charts and tables for the "Energy Profile" under "Environmental" pillar.

 

AI generated text for the Energy Profile

 

AI generated tables for the Energy Profile

 

In a similar manner, let us assign the reference facts and metrics to generate text , charts and tables for the "Water Profile" under "Environmental" pillar.

 

AI generated text for the Water Profile

 

AI generated chart for Water Usage by Business Country

 

  AI generated chart for Water Usage Over Time

 

Pillar: Social

In a similar manner, let us assign the reference facts and metrics to generate text , charts and tables for the "Health & Safety" under the "Social" pillar.

 

AI generated text for the Health & Safety facts

 

AI generated graph for the work related incidents & fatalities

 

Pillar: Governance

In a similar manner, let us assign the reference facts and metrics to generate text , charts and tables for the "Governance Body" under the "Governance" pillar.

 

AI generated text for the governance body metrics

 

AI generated graph for the metric share of the women in management

 

AI generated graph for the Anti-Corruption Incidents metrics

 

Following the successful setup of the sections and subsections for the ESG reporting content, let us take a look at the configured report content preview. Let us use the option "Preview" to understand the structure of the ESG report.

 

 

Now, let us generate the ESG report  using the tailored custom template. 

 

Once the details have been provided to generate the report using the tailored custom template,  select the "Generate Report" option.

 

While the ESG report is being generated, system displays the progress of the report.

 

Once the system generates the report, its status is updated as "Completed".

Let us select the option "Download" to view the ESG report in Pdf format.

The ESG report has been downloaded in .pdf format.

 

 

Here we go....

AI generated ESG report - SAP SCT

 

 

😊 This brings me to the end of the process walk-through of the creation of the custom ESG template and AI usage to generate tailored ESG report for a specific sustainability reporting year. I hope my blog proves useful to the sustainability team. I look forward to leveraging AI capabilities at scale to strengthen ESG reporting structures.

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy.C

entry.title: Re: Overall Performance Rating for Compensation Module -...
entry.content:
Hi DavidSomelofske,

maybe it helps if I add the following 2 screenshots.

My question would be, if the SAP standard process just allows 1 Performance Template and 1 Performance Rating, which is then stored in the Talent Profile.
And furthermore, the Compensation Processes have then to use this 1 Performance Rating for their processes? Or does the SAP standard process also allow you to have more Performance Templates and thus more Performance Ratings and the Comp Processes can use another Performance Rating than the one, which is published in the Talent Profile.

Our approach would be that the Overall performance Rating stored in the Talent Profile would be used for Succession and Talent Management. For the Compensation Processes we would have planned with a separate Performance Template and thus a separate PM-rating.

Many thanks.

Hello Luke, I have the same error. Where did you activated the item in the system for replenishment?

Thank you

entry.title: Sticky bit in Hana client

our Unix admins are doing OS hardening. They came up with the following files that have unacceptable permissions:

Many thanks!

entry.title: First smoke test after SAP LogServ solution deployment in Microsoft Sentinel - Part 0
entry.content:
Find your way to our central blog series entry here.

Dear community,

Due to the asynchronous integration between SAP LogServ and Microsoft Sentinel, it's advisable to perform a smoke test yourself before sharing your config data with SAP. This helps confirm if your receiving connector setup works and ensures the correct configuration will be shared with SAP the first time round.

 

Run a smoke test for ease of mind

Once the SAP LogServ push-connector is deployed on Microsoft Sentinel, you can start posting data towards its data collection endpoint. Use the provided REST client collection, or scripts to replicate the exact requests details that LogServ is performing too.

Maintain the variables as per your environment, see the LogServ sample payload and start posting. A working request shows http code 204 with no content on the response. See a sample output from the provided powershell script for reference. I prefer REST clients but people are different 😎

 

Find the parameters from the SAP LogServ Sentinel connector wizard on initial connect (see screenshot below). You can also get them directly from the individual resources. The data collection endpoint and data collection rule are created in the same resource group as your Log Analytics workspace underpinning Sentinel. They are prefixed with “SAPLogServ”. The app registration on Entra ID can be found from the Entra Application ID field.

 

Use the 2 KQL queries below to inspect your first data entry on the SAPLogServ_CL table in Sentinel.

SAPLogServ_CL
| take 10
SAPLogServ_CL
| summarize count() by clz_dir, clz_subdir
| order by clz_dir asc, clz_subdir asc

 

Wait a minute or two on first try. The population of new tables takes a short time initially.

 

Hurray you have a functioning data collection endpoint for the SAP LogServ data🎉

Now, continue your onboarding journey by sharing the config data with SAP over your chosen channel. Find the details on SAP's blog here.

What if you don’t see the data entry on the LogServ table?

  1. Verify the connection details: Did you really hit the right endpoint? Are you looking in the right tenant and log analytics workspace?
  2. Verify selection criteria: Is your Time range filter accurate? The smoke tests create entries for NOW or 30mins earlier in UTC.
  3. Did enough time pass on the initial table population? Consider a retry of the KQL query.
  4. Verify the Data collection rule has not been changed to filter all results or similar. See this blog part and KQL snippet for more details.

 

Final Words

That’s a wrap🌯. You saw today how to streamline your onboarding experience of SAP LogServ in Microsoft Sentinel. Simple smoke tests ensure a speedy onboarding and confidence in the shared setup configuration.

#Kudos to @Hemanth_Kusampudi and team for the partnership.

Get started with your deployment from here today.

Cheers Martin

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation – Email Task Not Renderi...
entry.content:
Hi @isushant07 , In the activity Send Email (Outlook), you need to set IsHtmlBody to true on the right side in the parameters. 

However, as per my knowledge, you can’t send a table or showcase a table using this activity, you can only send plain text.

entry.title: Re: Need help with SAP MAG - XSLT mapping context

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Spartagus not triggering OCC Source
entry.content: SSR Rendering was area not working well with Service Worker.

entry.title: Re: Onboarding Custom Task Permission Issue
entry.content:
Hello,

Is the custom mdf created for custom data collection or to be added to a hire template?

If is for Additional Data Collection task, I advise to check: Creating a Custom MDF Object for Custom Tasks

In particular:
3) Under the externalCode field, ensure that the Data Type is set to User.
5) ensuring that you always enter externalCode as Subject User Field.

See also Custom Tasks in Onboarding and Offboarding, towards the bottom of the Blog there are screenshots that shows how custom objects should look like.

Kind Regards,
Noemi

entry.title: Re: Top menu toolbar is not clickable
entry.content: I'm experiencing the same issue. I'm using a slightly less annoying workaround: pressing the Alt key before hovering the mouse over the menu. To make it work correctly, use the configuration below.

entry.title: Re: Need help with SAP MAG - XSLT mapping context
entry.content: @das_sappo not sure why it would map to another segment since E1EDL24 is the top level loop for items. Doing anything else would require scripted lookups by index or other means.

entry.title: Discover the Updated Data Model Guide
entry.content:
Navigating data models in SAP SuccessFactors can be complex, but we’re making it easier, together. We’re excited to introduce the updated Data Model Guide, now featured in the Getting Started section of the Employee Central Help Portal page. This update is designed to help you and your teams quickly access the foundational information needed to configure and manage your SuccessFactors environment. Whether you're new to data models or refining an existing setup, this guide serves as a collaborative resource to align your efforts, reduce complexity, and accelerate implementation.

The updated data model guide is available on both the Employee Central and Platform pages. We recognized that users often had to consult multiple sources to understand field behaviors, localization requirements, and configuration dependencies. To address this, we enhanced the guide, ensuring easier findability and accessibility to the information you need. Each section that was added to the guide plays a vital role in helping users configure and manage their SAP SuccessFactors environments more effectively.

For more information, refer to the Setting Up Data Models and Managing Business Configuration guide. 

 

Data Models

Understand the different types of data models available and how they can be utilized effectively.

What’s Changed and Why:

We’ve restructured the guide to make it easier to understand the different types of data models in SAP SuccessFactors—Corporate, Succession, and Country/Region-Specific. This new layout offers a more intuitive and holistic view of how these models work together to shape your system’s structure.

These data models are the foundation for configuring Employee Central and other modules. They define everything from job structures to employee records. By including this section in the guide, we’re helping users see how each model contributes to the overall behavior of the system. This clarity leads to more accurate and efficient configurations, fewer errors, and a smoother implementation experience.

 

Foundation Objects

Learn about the essential building blocks that support your data structure.

What’s Changed and Why:

We’ve added Foundation Objects in the Data Model Guide. These are the core elements; like departments, locations, and job classifications, that define your organizational structure in SAP SuccessFactors.

The updated guide makes it easier to understand and configure. It gives you a clearer, more connected view of how the system is structured. Understanding this section helps you to dive deeper into how these objects function, how they relate to one another, and how they can be adapted for different countries and business units.

 

Business Configuration UI

Get acquainted with the user interface that facilitates seamless configuration management.

What’s Changed and Why:

We’ve added the Business Configuration UI (BCUI) guide to the Data Model Guide. This intuitive interface allows administrators to manage configuration settings directly, without needing to work with XML files. It’s designed to simplify the process and make configuration more accessible to a wider range of users.

Including BCUI in the Data Model Guide reflects our commitment to making configuration easier and faster. It empowers not just technical teams, but also HR professionals and business users who may not have a technical background. By placing BCUI within the broader data model context, users can better understand how their configuration choices impact the system’s structure and behavior, leading to more accurate and efficient setups.

 

Field Information

Access links to detailed field information specific to Employee Central fields, as well as the Country/Region-Specifics guide.

What’s Changed and Why:

This section links to detailed documentation on Employee Central fields as well as Country/Region-Specific configurations. It’s an essential reference for implementers who need to understand field behavior, dependencies, and localization requirements.

By embedding this information directly in the guide, it eliminates the need for external lookups, streamlines the configuration process, enhances usability, and supports faster, more consistent decision-making—ensuring that localization and configuration nuances are easily accessible.

 

Share Your Feedback


We value your input! As you explore the updated Data Model guide, you'll notice a link to a short survey. We’d be incredibly grateful if you could take a few minutes to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback plays a vital role in helping us continuously improve and tailor our resources to better support your needs.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4/HANA Cloud Public, J58: Financial close - Bala...
entry.content:
Hello,

I hope below solution might help you.

There are many different common reasons for Balance Validation Failure in SAP J58. Its pretty hard to reach to conclusion unless we debugg the whole process using trace.

However, below are the common causes:-

A. Imbalance in Debit and Credit Entries

Total debit ≠ total credit → This is the most typical reason.

Check: Line items entered, especially manual ones or those uploaded via interface.

B. Incorrect GL Account Configuration

GL accounts might be set incorrectly (e.g. only debit allowed, open item managed, etc.).

Check: Transaction code FS00.

C. Currency Difference

Amounts in local vs. document currency can mismatch.

Check: Currency fields in the document header and line items.

D. Incorrect Posting Key Usage

Using wrong posting keys (like 40 for debit and 50 for credit in FI).

Check: T-code OB41 for posting key configurations.

E. Rounding Issues

Decimal rounding issues in exchange rates can throw balance validation off by a small amount.

Check: Document currency amounts and exchange rate precision.

F. Custom Validation or BADI/BTE Check

Custom enhancements (e.g., via BADI_ACC_DOCUMENT, BTE, or user exit) may be rejecting the document.

Check: Debug the script or ask ABAP team to trace the enhancement point.

Try to troubleshoot further to know the exact error:-

1. Run J58 in Simulation Mode (if available)

Look at the detail report or logs for imbalance data.

2. Use T-code FB03 or FB50 to Manually Simulate the Entry

Try recreating the document manually to isolate the problem.

3. Enable SAP Trace

Ask your SAP technical team to run a trace (ST05 or SM12) during the J58 run.

4. Check Logs

If using Batch Input Session, check logs via SM35.

 

Try above steps and let me know. Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: How to Create Service Request via Microsoft Outlook
entry.content:
I would like to know the automation of Service Request creation from incoming emails.

We have been testing and working with the Microsoft Outlook® Integration feature to convert emails sent to our Service Desk email address into follow-up Service Requests. 
We are looking to improve this process by leveraging the functionality outlined in the SAP document “Creating Service Requests From Incoming E-Mails.” - URL />
Our goal is to have Service Requests automatically created from emails sent to a specific email address (Support Desk), without requiring manual identification and conversion. This would allow us to manage and action the requests directly within the system, improving efficiency and turnaround time.

Please advise on the feasibility of this setup and any configuration steps required to enable this automation.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Create Service Request via Microsoft Outlook
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

You can refer to the below documents. 
Quick Guide for Microsoft Outlook® Integration
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2db278a3722d10149a069ccafaafb5d2.html#business-background

SAP ByDesign & Microsoft Outlook Integration – Part 1
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/0635ec3491974ad988be05d6b1dcf734/2de6544a722d10148803931c31af5ff9.html

KBA - 3345865 - How to Create Service Request via Microsoft Outlook 

Thanks & Regards, 
Arjun T H 
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Event-Based Revenue Recognition: Milestone Billing for Sell-from-Stock and Drop Shipment Processes
entry.content:
You can now take advantage of milestone billing in the public cloud for two important sales processes:

  • Sell from stock
  • Third-party drop shipment

This new option became available with release 2508.

What is milestone billing?

With milestone billing, billing and delivery are no longer tied together. That means:

  • You can create an invoice right after the sales order, even before anything is delivered.
  • The billed quantity doesn’t have to match the delivered quantity right away.

Supported Scenarios

  • Sell from stock
    • Use item category CBAO
    • Revenue recognition key: SFSCCM (Completed Contract Method)
  • Third-party drop shipment
    • Use item categories DB1 or DB2
    • Also with revenue recognition key SFSCCM

This works with Billing Relevance I (Order-Related Billing – Billing Plan).

Limitation

If the system derives the revenue recognition key SFSCCM automatically, you can’t switch to another revenue recognition key, except to or from NONREV (Comprehensive non-revenue recognition key).

TECO and Completion

An order reaches TECO status when:

  • The ordered, delivered, and invoiced quantities match and
  • Delivery, order, and overall status = C (complete)

entry.title: Re: Profit Center segments in S/4HANA public cloud - SSCU...
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,

A brief update confirming the solution for this question... 

As per the SSCUI excel spreadsheet provided in SAP note '3381770 - Delete entries from configuration activity (SSCUI)'

https://me.sap.com/notes/3381770/ESAP

you cannot delete Segments. 

For this particular implementation, the customer agreed that they only need one Segment per Co.Code (1110, 1120 and 1130).  We therefore amended the descriptions for 1000_A, 1000_B and 1000_C from: 

Segment A, Segment B and Segment C

to:

Segment 1110, Segment 1120 and Segment 1130

assigning these respectively to Profit Centers for the 3 Co.Codes. 

Kind regards,

Jules

entry.title: How to set up ASR for inbound in only one EWM warehouse, and not for all EWM warehouses?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm implementing advanced shipping and receiving in de demo system of my company. I would like to use EWM inbound document type IFO (mandatory for ASR) only for one warehouse. But the mapping of delivery types ERP-EWM is warehousenumber independent.

Does anybody know how to set this up?

entry.title: Re: How to assign roles to Consolidation steps ?
entry.content:
Hi @Titu,

Only 4 eyed check principle can applied for each of these steps.

You can also use authorization group for each of the steps to mimic agent behavior.

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: ABSENCE QUOTA CARRY OVER
entry.content: Yes, Calendars and cumulation periods are already generated until 2030.

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between Replicate Runtime Hier...
entry.content:
Hello @wangxlbest ,

I hope below solutuion might help you.

1. Verify Hierarchy in App “Manage Global Hierarchies”:

Go to Fiori App: Manage Global Hierarchies (App ID: F2913)

Search for hierarchy ID YPS2.

2. Confirm that it exists and is active.

Use Global Hierarchy Directly in Reports:

Since it's a global hierarchy now, reports like Contribution Margin Report or financial statement apps will read directly from the global hierarchy structure.

No replication is needed anymore.

3. Avoid Replication Job for Global Hierarchies:

Do not use J55 2.5.3 or similar runtime replication jobs for global hierarchies.

These are only applicable to non-migrated, legacy-style runtime hierarchies.

4. Update Documentation or Configuration Steps:

If you're following a SAP Best Practice or IMG Activity that includes replication, skip or adjust that step when the hierarchy has been migrated.

If issue still persists, I would encourage you to refer SAP Note 3355170 for solution to your issue.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: Re: You Won't Believe Where ZLLM's Documentation Lives (And How It Got There)
entry.content: @SergioFerrariIt2 tnx! fixed ^_^

entry.title: Error F5353 in FAGL_YEC_POSTINGS_EHP4
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm currently working on the year-end closing and encountered error F5353 for one of the G/L accounts.

"Postings in account 6100110 (company XXXX) are allowed only in USD currency
Message No F5353

Diagnosis
The Co.Ge account "6100110" is managed in &V3. Registration in "EUR" is therefore not possible.

Procedure
If a gain or loss in exchange should be recorded, the foreign currency valuation function must be used.
Perform function
To avoid this, correct the entry or modify the "USD" split key in the Co.Ge. "6100110" personal account of the company "XXXX"."

Could anyone clarify why SAP is attempting to post in the company code currency instead of the G/L account currency? For context, this particular G/L account has already been revaluated.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue? Any insights would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: ABSENCE QUOTA CARRY OVER
entry.content: I have checked the Generation Rules as well. Couldn't find any cause as to why the quota is not getting generated for 2028. Any other suggestions here, please?

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam Topics
entry.content: @Jan-WillemK I'm afraid that we have limited capacity to create and maintain CodeJam topics.  While this is a very good idea for a topic, it wouldn't be something we can add in the near future.  We are currently focused on adding new topics around Joule Studio and Joule for Developers in the near term.

entry.title: CAP: "cds deploy --to hana --no-build" no longer works with @sap/cds-dk 9.0.6
entry.content:
Hello experts,

cds deploy --to hana --no-build

 is no longer working with  @SAP/cds-dk 9.0.6.

Error message:

One or more options are not supported by cds deploy to hana: --no-build

With @SAP/cds-dk  8.6.1 it is working.

Output of cds deploy --help for both versions:

SYNOPSIS

cds deploy [ <model> ] [ <options> ]

--no-build (only in combination with --to hana)

 EXAMPLES
cds deploy --to sqlite:db
cds deploy --to hana
cds deploy --to hana:myService --auto-undeploy
cds deploy --to hana --no-build

entry.title: Re: Follow-Up Material Not Considered In MRP Run
entry.content:
Hello @Caetano,

Earlier attached snaps are for Follow up material that's why it was not having discontinued line in MD04 screen. I have attached the snap of discontinued material which has this line. Pls check and let me know if you required any other details.

entry.title: Re: Instance creation for apiportal-apiaccess plan in btp...
entry.content: I have no idea. It is random and luck. Only SAP product team can explain

entry.title: Re: Authentication methods CPI DS Agent
entry.content: When you connect to target system from DS, DS acts as client and you must supply credentials. Agents can not do that for you.

entry.title: Re: Increase Alternative bank account number field S4HANA...
entry.content:

thank you for your answer, but unfortuanetly this does not quite answer my question. 

The problem ist not the bank account number, but the ALTERNATIVE bank account number. This is currently 24 digits long, which is too short. Here is a Screenshot:

Do you know anything about that?

Kind regards,
Merle

entry.content: This is an excellent Blog ! This certainly helps with activation of RISE dashboard .

entry.title: Re: New features in SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.5
entry.content: Hi, is this only available for IOS devices?

entry.title: Re: Recruiting Posting Job Board Setup: eFinancial
entry.content:
Hello,

I know this is a question almost a year. 

Answering it though here if you haven't had the response before. Jobs and job boards configuration are not processed on Recruiting Posting preview environment. This is expected that some job board configurations cannot be configured and it is also expected that jobs are staying in "processing" status as 99% of the feed are not processed in preview. Only a couple of job boards have feeds in preview environment.

You can find those answers on the following KBAs : 

https://me.sap.com/notes/3245771/E

https://me.sap.com/notes/2721903/E

Valentin

entry.title: Dynamic Parameters - Windows 2022 server with Crystal Reports 2016 and Business Objects 4.2
entry.content:
Hello,

We have set up a new QA environment on a Windows 2022 server with Crystal Reports 2016 and Business Objects 4.2. Is someone able to confirm that the dynamic parameters should work when creating the reports? Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Issue with creating any sort of model in modeler with...
entry.content: Seems the same happened in the past and @achab worked on it. Not sure who is the current direct contact dealing with Free Trial version in SAP though https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sac-trial-unable-to-create-model-based-on-csv-or-xlsx-files/qaq-p/12728534

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Gantt Chart Custom Widget for SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Thanks for sharing.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content:
Hello @rpuranik, 

We already propose the "BI Automation Framework" : 

For automatic UNV to UNX conversion and bulk re-pointing (WebI), the BI Automation Framework, provided by default in SAP BusinessObjects (via the CMC), can help automate repetitive tasks efficiently. I recommend the following articles for more details:
    1. SAP Help Portal – BI Automation Framework
    2. SAP Community – Automation Framework Concept
    3. SAP Community – BI Automation Framework Samples

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Embedded Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) Advanced Production Integration Configurati
entry.content:
Hi @lingaiahvanam,

In a Brownfield ECC project for S/4HANA, a customer using WM, what configurations should be ensured for this transition to EWM? 

Configurations still in ECC and then in S4.

 

Best Regards,

Daniel

entry.title: AI Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FSP03
entry.content:
In this blog post, let's delve into the innovations delivered with Feature Pack Stack 03 for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, which was released in March 2025. We will focus on the cutting-edge innovations powered by artificial intelligence, revolutionizing how businesses operate and thrive. For an in-depth overview of the release, check out the latest update from our expert @Harish_Mangtani and his blog on this Cloud ERP solution.

The recent release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition introduces over 300 innovations, further enhancing capabilities across diverse business lines and industries. Key highlights include:

  • 200+ Innovations for Lines of Business
  • 60+ Innovations for Industry-Specific Needs
  • Numerous Localization and Cross-Topic Improvements

 

Join us as we explore how these innovations, especially in artificial intelligence, are setting new benchmarks in digital transformation, enabling businesses to harness data-driven insights for optimized decision-making and strategic growth.

Don’t miss this quick video that highlights some key innovations showcased in this post.

Please note that the following video was produced with Synthesia, a new platform that allows users to create videos featuring a digital twin of yourself and even includes a personal AI-generated Voice for an authentic touch.

  • Faster access to updates – Videos are created more frequently, so you're always getting the latest and most accurate information
  • Engaging and accessible – Short, to-the-point videos
  • More inclusive – Videos include subtitles and can be consumed in multiple languages

 

Watch now and let us know what you think in the comments!

Product Highlights

As enterprises look towards the future, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS03 leads the charge with seven groundbreaking AI innovations. These advancements are designed to streamline operations, enhance financial accuracy, and optimize resource management, setting the stage for effective and sustainable business transformation.

1. AI-Assisted Journal Uploads

Finance departments can now leverage generative AI for journal uploads, automating the creation and validation processes. By assigning guidance documents and automatically generating posting proposals, businesses ensure accuracy and compliance. This allows financial professionals to pivot their focus towards strategic financial planning, supported by a reliable audit trail. 

 

 

 

2. AI-Assisted In-House Service Initiation
Repair processing becomes seamless with AI extracting vital data from scanned documents. The system reads these documents, proposes necessary repairs, and reduces manual efforts significantly. Automated identification of equipment further enhances efficiency, offering a higher degree of accuracy and reducing the likelihood of errors in repair processing.

 


3. Joule-Enabled Mass Modification of Sales Orders

Sales Order Management sees significant enhancement using Joule's Copilot capabilities. Performing mass changes to sales orders is now faster and more efficient, as users can update multiple orders simultaneously by applying a simple command. This innovation minimizes the need for manual updates and enhances overall efficiency in sales order management.

 


4. AI-Based Explanation Assistant for Detailed Scheduling

For production planning, the AI-based Explanation Assistant offers clear insights into scheduling results. It provides real-time feedback and detailed summaries, enabling production planners to understand and trust the outcomes of the Detailed Scheduling Optimizer. This enhances transparency and ensures smooth operations, driving efficiency in production environments.

 

5. Project System Support with Joule Copilot Capabilities

Project management is revolutionized with Joule's new project assistant capabilities. Project system users can now access instant data and status updates using everyday language queries, eliminating the need for complex navigation in multiple systems. This innovation streamlines data consolidation, providing a comprehensive overview of a project’s progress with ease.

 

6. Joule-Enhanced Search for Bills of Materials

Selective Joule scenarios enhance exploration capabilities within Bills of Materials. Users can utilize simple, natural language queries to search product data efficiently. This is particularly beneficial for R&D engineers and change coordinators, enabling cost-effective product lifecycle management and increased flexibility in change-record operations.

 

7. Intelligent Vehicle Assignment in Automotive Management

In automotive vehicle management, intelligent assignment capabilities ensure optimal matching between vehicle configurations and sales orders. The intuitive app streamlines inventory management by optimizing vehicle assignments and facilitates swaps or unassignments efficiently, improving the overall processing speed.

 

For more details on the innovations mentioned, watch the video and use the chapter markers to skip directly to the topics you're most interested in.

Summary and what to do next

As you can see, we have a lot to offer, very much based on feedback of clients running their business on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

Thanks for your interest in this product update for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts across the Cloud ERP portfolio.

SAP S/4HANA  

 Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Re: GRC BRM - Digital signature enablement Option is not...
entry.content:
i meant to be with Access Request ARQ from below stage field

Also, you can review SAP KBA 2485296 Password required while approving request , and 
2176945 What system does the Role Reaffirm Approval authenticate against in AC 10?

For BRM, I don't see such options in delivered product.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4/HANA Embedded EWM Configuration - Blog 1 - step by step Guide - Organization structure
entry.content:
Hi @RaghavanKathamuthu ,

In a Brownfield ECC project for S/4HANA, a customer using WM, what configurations should be ensured for this transition to EWM? 

Configurations still in ECC and then in S4.

 

Best Regards,

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Recruiting Posting Job Board Setup: SeekSG
entry.content:
Hello,

I know this question was raised almost 1 year ago but please see my response below : 

It is the same response that for the job board efinancial. In preview environment the configuration is not actually live, therefore please note that the 2 fields that you can't field are directly linked to the "hirerID" field in the configuration. Therefore as there is no technical integration on preview environment, these fields cannot be filed because there is no API call made to retrieve the "Template" and "Advertisment Type" from your HirerID. This would be an expected behaviour.

Valentin

entry.title: Re: How to integrate Cloud ALM with external tickets system
entry.content: Vitor, excellent blog on connecting ServiceNow (SNOW) with SAP Cloud ALM — practical insights for enabling seamless integration and better IT service management.

entry.title: Re: Overall Performance Rating for Compensation Module -...
entry.content: The rating does not need to be stored in the Talent Profile, meaning the performance history background element in Employee Profile.   The rating can come directly from PM forms or other sources as mentioned previously.   It is up to the individual clients if they want to store the ratings after their performance process is completed.

entry.title: Re: Overall Performance Rating for Compensation Module -...
entry.content: This is based on compensation planning. Other modules may have different answers.

entry.title: Re: Unable to call Update API using http request
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Increase Alternative bank account number field S4HANA...
entry.content:
Hello Merle,

Thanks for clarifying.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the "Alternative Bank Account Number" field under House Bank Account Data is restricted to a maximum of 24 characters as per the standard data model (e.g., in the Manage Bank Accounts app and related APIs like API_BANK_ACCOUNT_SRV.

If you're encountering a value greater than 24 characters, it exceeds the standard SAP limit and must be handled via a workaround or correction.

Can you repurpose Street/City field from address and see if that can be leverage to concatenate further?

If you have genuine requirement supported by country's regulation, I would encourage you to reach out to SAP, if they can assist by any chance.


Appreciate your feedback.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: Advance payment invoices
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I would like to know if there is an APP - for SPA 4/H private cloud RISE - to manage the advance payments from customers to usefor italianian company.This APP must consider the advance payments invoices from customers without applying VAT (for customers that they are habitual exporters subject to declaration of intent to the Revenue Agency).The partner propose a custom to manage the advance payment in SD Module, but this custom doesn't maganed the VAT exsemption.

Thanks

Anastasia

entry.title: Multiple external systems connected to one SAProuter in SAP Private Cloud
entry.content:
We have SAP Private Cloud and we have added a SAProuter, and on the one hand we want to connect it to an external system for EDI, on the other hand we also have it for another system of connection with banks, but on the other hand we want to connect it to another external system of invoice management, but they tell us that it is not possible to connect to that SAProuter a third external system, honestly I thought that there were no limitations in connecting external systems to a single saprouter, I have been told that I would have to acquire a second saprouter, is this correct?

Regards

Andrés

Has anybody managed to get this please ?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Can someone help me with this question about SAP Sign...
entry.content:
As per my understanding, the correct options should be B, C, and D.
This is because:

  • B (Average customer wait time) helps identify delays and idle time in the process.

  • C (Time taken for each step of the application) allows us to analyze bottlenecks and optimize steps that take too long.

  • D (Number of applications processed per staff member) gives insight into workload distribution and resource efficiency, which directly impacts processing time.

entry.title: Re: The Future Has Been Written – the Rise of the Database Administrator

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

Mano.

entry.title: The PR/PO does not commit the budget
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have planned Budget for WBS C-25-OPD01-00CO-IN for 100,000 THB. 

When I created PO by specify WBS and amount is 599,999 THB. The commitment report doesn’t show commitment and not show warning budget. 

But I have created a supplier invoice, there is a warning as below.

Reference for Configuration

Best regards,

Keratiya D.

entry.title: Re: Advance payment invoices
entry.content:
Hello @Anastasia80 ,

 

Yes, in SAP (especially in S/4HANA or ECC), you can handle advance payments from customers who are habitual exporters (i.e., customers who are exempt from VAT under a declaration of intent) by configuring a suitable process using standard functions, often involving down payment requests (F-37) and down payment clearing (F-39)—but with special attention to VAT exemption handling.

Try to go through below solution step by step:-

1. Customer Master Data

In XD02 (or BP in S/4):

Set tax classification indicating exempt for VAT, based on the declaration.

Maintain the relevant VAT exemption reason code (used in Italy for "dichiarazione d’intento").

2. Tax Code Configuration (FTXP)

Create or verify a tax code with 0% VAT that includes:

Tax category = Output Tax

Rate = 0%

Tax exemption reason = declaration of intent (as per legal requirement)

3. Advance Payment Handling Options

A. Down Payment Request (F-37) + Incoming Payment (F-29)

Create a Down Payment Request without VAT.

Post the customer incoming payment with F-29, using special G/L indicator (e.g., "A" for down payment).

No VAT is posted here.

B. Billing Document (SD) Route with Exemption

If you use SD Billing:

Create a pro forma invoice or down payment invoice using a billing type like F2 or FAZ, linked to the exempt tax code.

Make sure the billing document does not calculate VAT due to the 0% tax code.

Please ensure customer must be flagged as a habitual exporter.

 

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: set automatically case due date for Signavio Governance
entry.content:
Hello together,

is it possible to set automatically a due date for a whole case and not only for single tasks?

 

I want to report all cases regarding their due dates but need to set them for each case manually. sure if you only have a few it is okay, but i have to manage >50 per week.

 

Thanks for your support

Daniel

entry.title: Re: 세금계산서 발행 및 계산서일자 조정 요청
entry.content:
Machine translation:

Request to issue tax invoices and adjust invoice dates
Good morning.

You received an Invoice for the Go-live Experience Workshop for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition on June 10-12.

Three people attended and received each of the three Invoices.

However, a non-Invoice tax invoice is required for processing by your company.

Target Invoice No. : .....

Invoice Date : July 1

When checking traces it looks like it goes through and clicks Yes on first pop-up and Close on second one, but actually first pop-up is still open when reconnected to VM. So for some reason they are not recognized. I tried with Click and also Click Sync (because click sync was recorded by recorder). Non of these helped. Any other ideas? Thank you.

Second pop-up:

Yogesh

Hi Team,

 

Deleting users in bulk is a documented process, please refer the following SAP Note:

SAP Solution Support

entry.title: How to short automatic packing HU in putaway
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

Our client would like the system to automatically pack Handling Units (HUs) in an inbound delivery based on predefined packing instructions.

Current Packing Instruction Setup:

  • 1 P = 1 PC

  • 1 M = 40 PC

When receiving 150 PC, the system currently creates the following HU split:

  • HU1 = 30 PC

  • HU2 = 40 PC

  • HU3 = 40 PC

  • HU4 = 40 PC

However, the expected packing result should be:

  • HU1 = 40 PC

  • HU2 = 40 PC

  • HU3 = 40 PC

  • HU4 = 30 PC

How to maintain the system fills full HUs (40 PC per HU) first, and only assigns the remaining quantity (30 PC) to the last HU. Is there any configuration related to this?

 

Regards,

Melani

entry.title: Splash Screen - SAP BTP Mobile Service - Not Working - MDK 24.11.3
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am currently trying to implement the use of a Splash Screen in an application built on SAP BTP Mobile Service, but despite following all the reference documentation, I have not been able to get it to work correctly.

https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/getting-started/mdk/custom-client/app-icon-splash-screen.html

I used the following configuration to set up the launcher and splash screen icons and applied it in the MDKProject.json file as instructed.

I’m using the following configuration to load the launcher and splash screen icons. I'm also adding the image folder using the same names and path as specified in the documentation.

However, neither the launcher icon nor the splash screen background appear in the app, even though everything seems to be set up as described. The project was built successfully without errors.

@robinkuck @bill_froelich @Jitendra_Kansal78 

Thank you very much for your help. If any additional information is needed, I remain at your disposal.

So I want to prevent to create one POD per workcenter-resource combination and this seemed to work with the views.

entry.title: ATC - Sap readiness check for 2023 S4 - error The SAP-owned namespace /0SJ1P/ is in scope. SAP objec
entry.content:
Hello All - 

 

we are running ATC for custom objects in brownfield approach , we are getting error as below - 
The SAP-owned namespace /0SJ1P/ is in scope. SAP objects cannot be checked in this case.

 

these error are appearing , when we are running for VOFM routein .. 
now the challenge is  - /0SJ1P/ in table "TRNSPACE " is have following values - 

NAMESPACE /0SJ1P/

ROLE P
LICENSE
EDITFLAG X
SSCRFLAG
SAPFLAG X
GEN ONLY
MT TYPE

i ve checked multiple blogs , there has been no solution provide people has tried multiple things , but still a final solution and approach is missing .


thanks

entry.title: Re: Discount % in Goods Issue
entry.content:
Hi @Underdog ,

I assume you are entering prices and totals for the items in the Goods Issue. If you copy and paste or input the price and also enter the total manually, SAP will adjust the fields to match the total, using a discount if necessary. Normally, you input quantity and unit price, and SAP computes the total based on quantity multiplied by price. Below are two scenarios: in the first, the total has been entered manually, while in the second, it has not.



Check if that was your case. I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Publish views from POD's to other users
entry.content:
I see! Perhaps you can evaluate creating desktop shortcuts or browser bookmarks for your PODs, including WCs/Resources in the URL like the example below:

https://xxxxxxx.execution.eu20-prod.web.dmc.cloud.sap/cp.portal/site#DMEWorkCenterPOD-Display
?sap-ui-app-id-hint=sap.dm.dme.pod&/?=&WORKCENTER=DCBR-A1&RESOURCE=BR-ASSY001

Hope it helps! Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: SAP Information Steward Task Failing - "Invalid colum...
entry.content:
Hi Joe,

Thanks for the response.

I checked the database and found the following:

  1. The column IS_GEN_SOURCE_ROWID is present in the <TABLE_ALIAS>_SOURCE table.
  2. However, this column is missing in <TABLE_ALIAS>_FR, and I couldn't locate the <TABLE_ALIAS>_SR table in the database.
  3. Also, the " Delete Failed Data and Run " option is greyed out/ not available.

entry.title: Return of Inventory in Transit
entry.content:
Hello Expert, 

I have the following scenario:

I generated a transfer order with delivery confirmation for 10 units.

The Good issue was made with the 10 units from the source document and was Posted.

When the merchandise arrived at the warehouse, (PO Good Receipt) only 8 units were received.

The question is, how can I return the 2 units that are in transit?

entry.title: Cannot update Picklist after updating source document
entry.content:
Hi, 

i ve got a problem and i need help. I am increasing picked quantity (over the released quantity) on picklist via service layer so I need to increase quantity on Sales order.

For Info:
Article has no prescribed batches on document before PATCH request and is managed using batch numbers and warehouse has NO BinLocations (Disabled BinLocations) 

I do PATCH /b1s/v2/Orders(1111)

{
  "DocEntry": 1413,
  "DocNum": 1111,
  "DocObjectCode": "oOrders",
  "DocumentsOwner": 5,
  "SalesPersonCode": 1,
  "DocDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
  "DocDueDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
  "TaxDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
  "CardCode": "Test",
  "CardName": "Test Test",
  "NumAtCard": "",
  "PaymentGroupCode": 4,
  "ReserveInvoice": "tNO",
  "AddressExtension": {
    "ShipToStreet": "Test",
    "ShipToBuilding": "",
    "ShipToCity": "Praha",
    "ShipToZipCode": "111 11",
    "ShipToCountry": "CZ",
    "BillToStreet": "Test",
    "BillToBuilding": "",
    "BillToCity": "Praha",
    "BillToZipCode": "111 11",
    "BillToCountry": "CZ",
    "DocEntry": 1413
  },
  "DocumentLines": [
    {
      "LineNum": 0,
      "VisualOrder": 0,
      "LineStatus": "bost_Open",
      "BaseType": -1,
      "ItemCode": "B3002",
      "ItemDescription": "Desc",
      "ShipDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
      "WarehouseCode": "01",
      "UseBaseUnits": "tNO",
      "RemainingOpenQuantity": 5.0,
      "InventoryQuantity": 5.2,
      "RemainingOpenInventoryQuantity": 5.0,
      "U_VAG_WHP_OriginalQtty": 4.0,
      "MeasureUnit": "",
      "UnitsOfMeasurment": 1.0,
      "UoMEntry": -1,
      "UoMCode": "Manuálně",
      "BatchNumbers": [
        {
          "BatchNumber": "AAA",
          "BaseLineNumber": 0,
          "Quantity": 5.2,
          "SystemSerialNumber": 2,
          "ItemCode": "TestItem"
        }
      ],
      "SerialNumbers": [],
      "DocumentLinesBinAllocations": [],
      "DocumentLineAdditionalExpenses": []
    }
  ],
  "DocumentAdditionalExpenses": [],
  "DocumentSpecialLines": []
}

PATCH request will Succeed and after that i try to update picklist 
PUT  /b1s/v2/PickLists(679)
{
    "Absoluteentry": 679,
    "Name": "Manager",
    "ObjectType": "156",
    "OwnerCode": 1,
    "OwnerName": null,
    "PickDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
    "Status": "ps_Released",
    "UseBaseUnits": "tNO",
    "PickListsLines": [
        {
            "AbsoluteEntry": 679,
            "LineNumber": 0,
            "OrderEntry": 1413,
            "OrderRowID": 0,
            "PickedQuantity": 5.2,
            "ReleasedQuantity": 0,
            "BaseObjectType": 17,
            "SerialNumbers": [],
            "BatchNumbers": [
                {
                    "BatchNumber": "AAA",
                    "BaseLineNumber": 0,
                    "SystemSerialNumber": 2,
                    "Quantity": 5.2,
                    "ItemCode": "TestItem"
                }
            ],
            "DocumentLinesBinAllocations": []
        }
    ]
}

Result =>
{
    "error": {
        "code": "-2039",
        "details": [
            {
                "code": "",
                "message": ""
            }
        ],
        "message": "Another user or another operation modified data; to continue, open the window again (ODBC -2039)"
    }
}

When i do the same with warehouse with bin locations and with aditional /b1s/v2/PickListsService_UpdateReleasedAllocation call, request will be OK, 
if i do the same with warehouse without bin locations and without batches (only PATCH Orders, PUT Picklists) request will be ok. 
What i should do different. I tried it also with PickListsService_UpdateReleasedAllocation call (the same ERROR), i tried PATCH instead of PUT (the same ERROR).
If you ve got any advice, any solution, i will be glad. 
Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Prorate based on Months not days
entry.content: I tested this and it works perfect for list prices already in the Lookup table. We have the requirement that we need this also to work the same way for manual override prices in the subscription. If a change occure for a Yearly billing cycle jJanuary 1 to December 31st, and from April 1st the qty is changed. Then this proration does not work, only when using list price from the lookup table, not for the overridable price. Any suggestion for this?

entry.title: Transfer historical unbilled revenue to Enteprise project
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have a cost based POC project in EPPM and i'm trying to move historical unbilled revenue from older system to New public cloud.

Can you tell how can i create a GL account entry to add unbilled revenue against my project so that it shows in event based revenue recognition. Sharing the template in attachment

Thanks,

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP Customer Checkout Credit Card Refunds not shown in SAP B1 Deposits window
entry.content: Hi candrade_0518, SAP B1 provide the option to register an Incoming Payment with negative amounts based on a A/R Credit Memo or a negative A/R Invoice. So, by saying this it is possible in SAP B1 to do a credit card refund and been able to select it in the Deposit to bank window. The only thing here that I'm realizing is that SCCO integration is not doing that even if SAP B1 permits it by standard. Think about it, doing a gross deposit (without refunds) and afterwards in the bank reconciliation start looking for all credit card refunds that should be part of different deposits will be an uphill and difficult approach

entry.title: Re: connecting to success factors from SOAP API fails
entry.content: Hi Cyril, yes, it was the date range and data available, got it fixed thank you.

entry.title: Re: Budget scenarion
entry.content:
Hello,

I do see you are using the Correct Fiori App. "Manage Budget Scenarios" (App ID: F2129)

Check other settings and do some due diligence as below:-

A. Check Roles and Authorizations - If its an authorization issue.

You need roles like:

SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT

SAP_BR_BPC_CONTROLLER

SAP_BR_OVERHEAD_ACCNTNT

Use the "Maintain Business Roles" app to check if the user has these roles.

B. Check for Missing Configuration Steps

Besides initializing, verify:

1. Ledger and Controlling Area settings are correctly linked.

2. Fiscal Year Variant is properly set.

3. Budget Profile and Version are defined.

4. Controlling Area is not correctly assigned to company codes.

5. Planning versions (like 0 or 1) are not active.

I hope this helps you to resolve the issues.

 

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: SQ02 join LIPS and EKPO
entry.content:
Hello

I need to provide a query to business who needs to check inbound deliveries at item level with some specific data that are only at PO item level

LIPS.VGPOS and EKPO.EBELP cannot be jointed because I guess there is a format issue

Does anybody know how to correctly add code inside SQ02 in order to force this joint (I tried in DB02 and the SQL code works fine)

I tried some silly code like this but it does not work

Do you know which section I should use and how to write a better code please ?

Should I also add the table here ?

Thank you very much

entry.title: Re: how to throw message in webdynpro
entry.content:
Hi Simon,

This works perfectly! I double checked the allowed method for View parameter and now understood what you meant. Thank you so much for your help!

entry.title: fixed text ME53N
entry.content: Text of the destination deposit field in the RC, has the current information in S4 - as Supplier Center. We need it to be corrected to the correct information, which is Stock Output Warehouse. According to the attached file, we saw that this is a fixed text in the program, making it necessary to correct the translation of the text into Brazilian Portuguese

entry.title: Re: Name Correction
entry.content:
recorrected my  name   certification name is not the  right name assigned the paper 

the certification name say AYELIGN MEKONNEN  ---- MEKONNEN he is my  grand father not my father. so the middle name of my father TIGABU is missed from the paper. 

The right name is AYELIGN TIGABU MEKONNEN 

regards

entry.title: Re: Splash Screen - SAP BTP Mobile Service - Not Working...
entry.content: That feature was introduced in MDK 25.6.0.   Please upgrade to this version and try again.
